segments.text: Lesson 2.

translations.zh-Hans: 第2课。

segments.text: Thirteen Equals One.

translations.zh-Hans: 十三等于一。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Was the vicar pleased that the clock was striking?

translations.zh-Hans: 钟敲响了，牧师高兴吗？

segments.text: Why?

translations.zh-Hans: 为什么？

segments.text: Our vicar is always raising money for one cause or another, but he has never managed to get enough money to have the church clock repaired.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们的牧师总在为这样或那样的事业筹款，但他从未筹到足够的钱来修理教堂的大钟。

segments.text: The big clock, which used to strike the hours day and night, was damaged many years ago and has been silent ever since.

translations.zh-Hans: 这座大钟过去不分昼夜地按时报时，许多年前损坏以后便一直沉默至今。

segments.text: One night, however, our vicar woke up with a start.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而，一天夜里，牧师突然惊醒了。

segments.text: The clock was striking the hours.

translations.zh-Hans: 大钟正在报时。

segments.text: Looking at his watch, he saw that it was one o'clock, but the bell struck thirteen times before it stopped.

translations.zh-Hans: 他看了看手表，发现是一点钟，但钟敲了十三下才停。

segments.text: Armed with a torch, the vicar went up into the clock tower to see what was going on.

translations.zh-Hans: 牧师拿着手电筒登上钟楼，想看看究竟发生了什么事。

segments.text: In the torchlight, he caught sight of a figure whom he immediately recognized as Bill Wilkins, our local grocer.

translations.zh-Hans: 借着手电筒的光，他看见一个人影，马上认出那是本地的杂货商比尔·威尔金斯。

segments.text: Whatever are you doing up here, Bill?

translations.zh-Hans: “比尔，你到底在上面干什么？”

segments.text: asked the vicar in surprise.

translations.zh-Hans: 牧师惊讶地问。

segments.text: I'm trying to repair the bell, answered Bill.

translations.zh-Hans: “我在设法修钟，”比尔回答说。

segments.text: I've been coming up here night after night for weeks now.

translations.zh-Hans: “几个星期以来，我每天夜里都到这儿来。

segments.text: You see, I was hoping to give you a surprise.

translations.zh-Hans: 你瞧，我原想给你一个惊喜。”

segments.text: You certainly did give me a surprise, said the vicar.

translations.zh-Hans: “你确实给了我一个惊喜，”牧师说。

segments.text: You've probably woken up everyone in the village as well.

translations.zh-Hans: “你恐怕也把村里所有人都吵醒了。

segments.text: Still, I'm glad the bell is working again.

translations.zh-Hans: 不过，我很高兴这钟又能走了。”

segments.text: That's the trouble, vicar, answered Bill.

translations.zh-Hans: “问题就在这里，牧师，”比尔回答说。

segments.text: It's working all right.

translations.zh-Hans: “它走得倒是没问题。

segments.text: But I'm afraid that at one o'clock it will strike thirteen times, and there's nothing I can do about it.

translations.zh-Hans: 可我担心每到一点钟它就会敲十三下，而我对此无能为力。”

segments.text: We'll get used to that, Bill, said the vicar.

translations.zh-Hans: “我们会习惯的，比尔，”牧师说。

segments.text: Thirteen is not as good as one, but it's better than nothing.

translations.zh-Hans: “十三下虽不如一下好，但总比一下也不敲强。

segments.text: Now let's go downstairs and have a cup of tea.

translations.zh-Hans: 现在我们下楼去喝杯茶吧。”
